narrative.context: Two friends argue. One seems angry, the other seems withdrawn. Both ask you to take sides.
narrative.prompt: What understanding helps you respond effectively?

options.label: Identify differing emotional needs
options.description: See anger may signal feeling unheard; withdrawal may signal overwhelm or hurt. Offer to listen separately without taking sides.

ei.rationale: Understands how different emotions map to needs and context; supports balanced, effective response.

options.label: Assume both are being immature
options.description: Tell them to stop fighting without exploring what’s underneath.

ei.rationale: Reduces immediate tension, but misses deeper emotional causes; partial understanding.

options.label: Pick the calmer person as 'right'
options.description: Assume the angry person is wrong and side with the withdrawn friend.

ei.rationale: Equates visible emotion with correctness; misunderstands emotional meaning.

options.label: Spread the conflict
options.description: Tell others about it and intensify group pressure.

ei.rationale: Escalates and ignores emotional dynamics; undermines repair.
